mainText:
**Ukraine’s Deep-Strike Strategy**
**By Evan Spear, James P. Sutton, Daniel Sipes, Ross Anderson | July 13, 2026**

**Lindsey Graham Dies at 71**
Senator Lindsey Graham, the senior Republican senator from South Carolina, died on Saturday at the age of 71 after a “brief and sudden illness,” according to his office. A preliminary report from the Washington, D.C., medical examiner’s office determined that Graham died of an aortic dissection, a tear in the aorta, the main artery carrying blood from the heart. Graham had returned from a trip to Ukraine on Saturday, after which emergency workers responded to a call about someone experiencing chest pains at Graham’s Washington address.

Elected in 2002 to replace former Senator Strom Thurmond, Graham briefly ran for president in 2016, served as head of the Senate Judiciary Committee during President Donald Trump’s first term, and was chair of the Senate Budget Committee at the time of his death. He was a consistent advocate for U.S. support for Ukraine in its war against Russia and supported military actions against Iran.  South Carolina Governor Henry McMaster will appoint a replacement to serve out the remainder of Graham’s term, and the party’s 2026 nominee for his seat will be decided through a special election set for August 11.

**More Iran-U.S. Strikes Over the Weekend**
Over the weekend, the U.S. launched three waves of air strikes against Iran in retaliation for Iranian attacks on merchant shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. The strikes targeted missile and air-defense systems, as well as small attack boats near the strait. U.S. Central Command confirmed that these strikes aimed to degrade Iran's ability to attack civilian mariners and commercial ships. A larger round of strikes the night prior hit approximately 140 targets.

Iranian forces disabled the Cyprus-flagged M/V GFS Galaxy with missiles and drones, resulting in one crew member missing at sea. Additionally, on Saturday, Omani negotiators proposed a two-lane approach to managing the Strait of Hormuz, allowing the southern route to remain open to all shipping without restrictions. As a result of the ongoing tensions, Brent crude prices rose more than 4 percent to $79.26 a barrel, the highest since June 22.

**Trump Fires Members of Federal Election Commission**
On Thursday, President Donald Trump removed all three sitting members of the federal Election Assistance Commission (EAC). The EAC, a bipartisan agency, assists states in administering elections but has no direct authority over them. The president fired the two Democratic commissioners and asked the Republican commissioner to resign. A White House official indicated that all three positions will be replaced, although each is subject to Senate confirmation.

**Apple Sues OpenAI, Accusing It of IP Theft**
Apple has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI, its io Products hardware unit, and specific individuals, accusing them of stealing trade secrets and confidential information. The lawsuit alleges that former Apple employees attempted to take proprietary technology from Apple for OpenAI's device development. Apple seeks an injunction to prevent OpenAI from using its trade secrets and is demanding compensatory damages.

**British Police Make Arrest in Suspected Murder of Former Minister**
British police announced the arrest of a 28-year-old man in South Yorkshire on suspicion of murdering Ann Widdecombe, a former Conservative MP. Widdecombe was found dead at her home in Devon, a day after appearing on TalkTV. If confirmed as murder, she would be the third prominent British politician murdered since 2016. Police have stated that there is no current evidence suggesting the incident is terrorism-related.

**Today in America**
The 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act became law on Saturday without President Trump’s signature due to his protest over the Senate's failure to pass a nationwide voter ID bill. Victor Marx won the Republican primary for Colorado governor, defeating state Senator Barbara Kirkmeyer. Wally Funk, a female aviator who passed NASA-style tests in 1961 but was denied astronaut status, died at 87 after reaching space in 2021.
